decisions_contributed: Decompose implementation into four user-story phases plus setup and final verification: Settings template management, import/export, empty chat workflow start, and non-empty linked workflow start.
Treat server workflow template registry and mutation API as the foundation for Settings and Chat consumers.
Make tests-first tasks explicit for every production surface under src/server and desktop/src.
Keep import/export independent from basic CRUD because preview, selected commit, rename conflicts, and unknown-field preservation are independently testable.
Split non-empty linked workflow start from empty chat workflow start because CA-005 carries separate context strategy and source-preservation obligations.
join_points: JP1 after T007: server template registry and CRUD API contract are stable enough for desktop Settings work.
JP2 after T011: Settings CRUD is independently validatable.
JP3 after T015: import/export is independently validatable.
JP4 after T017: empty/new chat workflow start is independently validatable.
JP5 after T023: linked non-empty chat start is validatable across server and desktop.
JP6 after T024: feature-level quality evidence is assembled.
integration_notes: T021 is the highest-risk implementation task because summarize must use non-mutating compact-style semantics.
All production changes under src/server and desktop/src require same-area tests.

Forbidden drift includes project-level templates, built-in direct edits/deletes, overwrite-by-default imports, prompt-only phases, source-session /compact mutation, retroactive snapshot mutation, and coverage gate bypass.
